transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` â€” make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` â€” list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` â€” bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` â€” show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` â€” dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` â€” merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` â€” drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` â€” list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` â€” resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` â€” substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: â€¦"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict â€” it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmp5hF6id
Scenario seed: 9378412744764439511

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): shared/file-0.txt
3. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-1` based on the project's default branch.
4. Commit pending changes in workspace `ws-0` with a descriptive message.

# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. - `maw ws diff <name>` â€” show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.

- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` â€” merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` â€” drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).

- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` â€” resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.

```
maw exec alice -- git status
```

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict â€” it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

turns.reply_text: I'll start by inspecting the workspace structure, then complete the task battery.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace create requires an explicit source.
  Use one of:
    maw ws create --from main ws-0
    maw ws create --change <change-id> ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 from main"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-0' at ws/ws-0 ...

Workspace 'ws-0' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  a72f8a09338a (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-0/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-0/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-0 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

turns.reply_text: Now I'll check the shared directory structure and create the file to edit.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"mkdir -p /tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-0/shared && echo \"coordination edit for ws-0\" > /tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-0/shared/file-0.txt","description":"Create shared/file-0.txt with content"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-1","description":"Create workspace ws-1 from main"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-1' at ws/ws-1 ...

Workspace 'ws-1' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  a72f8a09338a (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-1/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-1/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-1 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-1 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

turns.reply_text: Now I'll commit the pending changes in ws-0.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git add shared/file-0.txt && maw exec ws-0 -- git commit -m \"feat: add shared/file-0.txt for coordination task\"","description":"Stage and commit changes in ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
[detached HEAD e5e1f14] feat: add shared/file-0.txt for coordination task
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)
 create mode 100644 shared/file-0.txt

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default	/tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/default
ws-0	/tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-0 (ready to merge) [lifecycle:committed-unintegrated]
ws-1	/tmp/.tmp5hF6id/ws/ws-1 [lifecycle:clean]

Merge ready: maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

turns.reply_text:
All tasks complete:

1. **ws-0 created** from `main`
2. **`shared/file-0.txt` edited** in ws-0 with content "coordination edit for ws-0"
3. **ws-1 created** from `main`
4. **Changes committed** in ws-0 with message "feat: add shared/file-0.txt for coordination task"

Both workspaces exist; ws-0 is ready to merge, ws-1 is clean.

done
